skill.name: google-drive-automation
skill.description: Automate Google Drive upload, download, search, sharing, folder, and metadata workflows through the Rube MCP Google Drive toolkit.
skill.summary: Run Google Drive file, folder, search, and sharing workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and reconnaissance matches are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, tool names, MIME types, and parameter names. The real risk is operational: the skill connects to an external MCP endpoint, authorizes Google Drive access, transfers files, and can change sharing permissions, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Handles Google Drive Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authorize the Google Drive toolkit. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it gives a third-party MCP integration access to Drive workflows and OAuth-mediated actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external MCP URL and Google OAuth setup are explicitly documented. The risk is operational exposure through an integration, not evidence of malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Drive Sharing and Permission Changes Require Care
medium_findings.description: The skill supports creating, updating, and deleting Google Drive permissions, including domain, public, and ownership-related sharing options. Incorrect prompts or weak review could expose private files or grant excessive access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed permission tools and parameters clearly support access changes. The skill also warns about risky sharing, so the concern is controlled but real.

medium_findings.title: File Upload and Download Workflows Move User Data
medium_findings.description: The skill documents upload, download, export, and temporary URL workflows for Drive files. These actions can move sensitive user content between Google Drive, internal storage, and temporary download locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Upload, download, internal storage, and temporary URL handling are explicitly described. This is expected functionality, but it requires user confirmation and data-handling caution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many inline backticks as Ruby or shell execution. In context, they are Markdown formatting for tool names, parameters, and examples, with no executable script block or shell command invocation found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose and Markdown inline code only. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, shell execution, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary prose, Drive query parameters, MIME types, and permission tool names. No cryptographic operation, hash routine, host probing, or system inspection logic appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows documentation strings and Google Drive tool references. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Rube MCP URL Is Expected Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill includes a fixed Rube MCP server URL. This is a real network dependency, but it is disclosed as setup configuration and does not by itself show covert exfiltration or malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is plainly present and required for setup. Its purpose is documented, so the risk is limited to dependency trust and network access.

content.value_statement: Manual Drive work can be slow and error-prone when files, folders, and permissions change often. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable workflow guide for Google Drive operations through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for the Rube MCP Google Drive toolkit.
Documents upload, resumable upload, download, and export workflows.
Shows how to search files by name, type, owner, folder, and date filters.
Explains folder creation, file moves, parent IDs, and verification steps.
Outlines permission listing, sharing, updates, revocation, and contact lookup.
Lists common Drive API pitfalls for pagination, shared drives, MIME types, and rate limits.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and Google Drive authorization.
Does not provide standalone code or a local Google Drive client.
Basic uploads are limited to files already available in internal storage.
Permission changes and public sharing need explicit user review before execution.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Delivery Folders
use_cases.description: Create folders, upload final assets, verify metadata, and share reader access with the correct client contacts.

use_cases.title: Find and Export Drive Reports
use_cases.description: Search Drive by file type, date, or owner, then export Google Docs, Sheets, or Slides in a requested format.

use_cases.title: Audit Drive Permissions
use_cases.description: List permissions for selected files, identify broad access, and revoke or narrow permissions after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Find a File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google Drive automation skill to find files named "quarterly report" in my Drive. Show the matching names, IDs, MIME types, and modified dates before taking any other action.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Folder and Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a folder named "Client Review" under the project folder I provide, upload the prepared file from internal storage, and verify the final file metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Share With Reviewers
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the proposal file, list its current permissions, then share it as commenter with the reviewers I name. Ask for confirmation before changing any permission.

prompt_templates.title: Export and Organize Reports
prompt_templates.prompt: Search all accessible drives for monthly reports modified after 2026-01-01, export Google Sheets as Excel files, and organize the exports by department folder. Use pagination and report any skipped files.

output_examples.input: Find Drive PDFs with "invoice" in the name and summarize the matches.
output: A concise list of matching PDF files with file names, IDs, owners, and modified dates.
A note when pagination or shared drive access may affect completeness.
A recommendation to verify the target file before download or sharing.

output_examples.input: Share the budget sheet with finance@example.com as reader.
output: The current permission summary for the selected budget sheet.
A confirmation request before adding the reader permission.
A post-change verification summary after the permission is applied.

output_examples.input: Move files from one folder to another and confirm the result.
output: Resolved source and destination folder IDs.
A list of moved files and any files that could not be moved.
Metadata checks showing the final parent folder state.

best_practices: Resolve file and folder IDs before running upload, move, download, or sharing actions.
Ask for explicit confirmation before public sharing, domain sharing, ownership transfer, or deletion of permissions.
Use pagination, scoped fields, and smaller queries for large drives or shared drive searches.
anti_patterns: Do not set export MIME types when downloading non-Google files such as PDFs or Excel files.
Do not rely on folder names alone for nested paths; use resolved folder IDs.
Do not create broad "anyone" or domain permissions without a clear business reason and user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide for using Rube MCP Google Drive tools.

faq.question: What connection is required?
faq.answer: Users need Rube MCP configured and an active Google Drive connection for the googledrive toolkit.

faq.question: Can it upload large files?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents standard uploads and a resumable upload fallback for larger files.

faq.question: Can it change sharing permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers adding, listing, updating, and deleting permissions, so confirmation is important.

faq.question: Does it search shared drives?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents shared drive listing and all-drives search options when OAuth scopes allow access.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it can move Drive files and change access through an external MCP integration.
